description:
Working directory: /Users/bryanw/Projects/Vintage/tools/sourcerer
Task packet: .ai/tasks/sourcerer-v2a-20260319160020-llm-extended-analysis/

Extend the LLM post-disassembly analysis plugin with three additional review passes to improve generated source documentation:

PASS 1 — DATA-AS-CODE: Scan DATA-typed regions for valid instruction sequences. Pass candidates with surrounding CODE context to LLM. LLM returns reclassify_as_code annotations. Apply by setting AddressType::CODE and triggering a re-disassembly of those regions before formatting.

PASS 2 — BYTES-AS-STRINGS: Scan DATA regions for runs of printable ASCII (0x20-0x7E, 4+ consecutive). Pass sequences to LLM for confirmation. Annotate confirmed strings with formatter-appropriate directive (FCC for EDTASM, ASC/STR for Merlin, .BYTE for SCMasm). Comment with the actual string content.

PASS 3 — CODE PATTERN RECOGNITION (heuristic + LLM): Identify common vintage code idioms and label/comment them. Patterns include:
  - String ops: copy, compare, search, length (inner loop with LDA/STA/INX/BNE or similar)
  - Graphics: pixel plot, line draw, fill, sprite blit, HIRES/LORES detection (Apple II), screen clear
  - Math: software multiply, divide, BCD arithmetic, 16-bit add/subtract
  - Memory: block move, block clear/fill, byte swap
  - I/O: input polling loops, keyboard scan, printer output, disk access prologue/epilogue
  - Control: dispatch tables, jump tables, state machines
  - Interrupt handlers: ISR entry/exit patterns (PHP/PHA prologue, PLA/PLP/RTI epilogue)
  LLM receives the code block with context (platform, CPU, surrounding labels) and returns a suggested function name, one-line description, and per-instruction comments for key instructions.

All three passes run as additional steps inside ClaudeAnalyzer::Analyze() after the existing instruction annotation pass. Results feed into AddressMap labels and comments before the formatter runs. Passes should be individually enableable (flags or always-on is acceptable for initial implementation).

Key files: src/analysis/llm/claude_analyzer.h/.cpp, src/analysis/llm/chunk_builder.h/.cpp, src/analysis/llm/llm_analyzer.h, src/core/address_map.h, src/core/binary.h, src/core/instruction.h. Contract and plan must be written in task packet before coding.
title: LLM Analysis: Extended Review Passes for Source Documentation

description:
Working directory: /Users/bryanw/Projects/Vintage/tools/sourcerer
Task packet: .ai/tasks/sourcerer-20260319155218-llm-data-analysis/

Extend the LLM post-disassembly analysis plugin (src/analysis/llm/) with two additional review passes:

1. DATA-AS-CODE DETECTION: Identify DATA-typed address regions that look like valid code sequences. Pass DATA blocks to the LLM alongside surrounding CODE context. LLM flags candidate regions with a 'reclassify_as_code' annotation. Apply by calling AddressMap::SetType(addr, AddressType::CODE) on confirmed candidates and re-running a disassembly pass.

2. BYTES-AS-STRINGS DETECTION: Scan DATA regions for runs of printable ASCII bytes (0x20-0x7E, 4+ consecutive). Pass candidate byte sequences to the LLM to confirm if they are human-readable strings. If confirmed, annotate with a 'string_data' comment and suggest using the assembler string directive (FCC/ASC/STR depending on formatter) instead of raw FCB/HEX bytes.

Both passes are additive to the existing Analyze() flow in ClaudeAnalyzer. They run after the existing instruction annotation pass. Results are written into AddressMap comments/types before the formatter runs.

See existing plugin: src/analysis/llm/claude_analyzer.h/.cpp, src/analysis/llm/llm_analyzer.h, src/core/address_map.h (AddressType::DATA, GetType, SetType), src/core/binary.h (GetPointer, size). Contract and plan should be created in task packet before implementation.
title: LLM Analysis: Data-as-Code and Bytes-as-Strings Detection
